economic_notes: Nation has business enterprises in Investment Holding & Management, Mining Services, and Retail & Commercial Management. COVID-19 impacted operations including reduced gaming revenues from First Nations Trust and distancing measures. ISC increased financial resources to ensure safe provision of services.
notes: Fiscal year is 2021, not 2024. Nation has 9 business enterprises accounted for by modified equity method (GBEs) across 3 industries. Three departments consolidated (Patuanak, La Plonge, CMHC Housing). English River Community Benefits Trust and Treaty Land Entitlement Trust excluded from consolidation as controlled by independent trustees. Loss from GBE investments of $1,386,903 included in revenue as negative line. Total government transfers estimated as ISC ($14,809,209) + CMHC ($243,694) + Government of Saskatchewan ($41,631) + Northern Lights School Division ($200,000) + Saskatchewan Indigenous Cultural Centre ($19,316) + Northern Sports Culture & Recreation ($61,953) = $17,375,803; however MLTC entities may also be government transfers bringing total higher. OSR calculated as total revenue minus estimated government transfers. COVID-19 significantly increased ISC funding in 2021.
